actions.value:
Hi,
thanks for reaching out.
The problem is that you send Matic tokens on Ethereum instead of Matic coins on Polygon.

In Wallets, long press on the Matic Polygon wallet you sent the tokens to, tap Split Ethereum.

You will see a new Ethereum wallet in Wallets list.

Now, in Wallets, long press on the new Ethereum wallet, Add / Edit token, search for Matic, flag it and Save.
You will see a new Matic Token wallet in Wallets list with your tokens inside.

​

Remember: if you need to move these Matic tokens in the future, you will need some fraction of ethereum to pay gas (mining fees).
This is an Ethereum network policy not an Edge one.

Please note that this policy applies also to exchanges, so if you want to exchange some ERC20 token to a coin (for example USDT to ETH), you need some ETH to cover the mining fees, because the exchange needs a normal Ethereum transaction.

Less than 10 USD in ethereum is usually sufficient to cover a lot of token transactions but the at the moment the Ethereum network is congested, so it could be impossible to move your tokens with a Standard or Low fee. 


You could try to change the network fees by tapping on the fee section at the send screen to custom however be aware that a failed transaction will result in a loss of the proposed fee. 


Please note that:
1. The transaction could be dropped
2. Fees could be even 10-20 USD per transaction

3. The more complex smart contracts are (eg CDAI), the higher the fees required will be

Please let me know if you have any questions.
